channel.title: Ozaia
channel.description: A quiet presence for every phase of a woman's life. Articles on silent anxiety, hormonal health, motherhood, and emotional wellbeing.

item.title: She Carries It Well. That Is the Problem.
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: Women are more than twice as likely as men to experience generalized anxiety disorder. High-functioning anxiety hides behind competence, devotion, and the inability to stop. Ozaia names the pattern.

item.title: The Anxiety No One Talks About at Fifteen
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: She gets straight A's. Her stomach hurts every morning. No one connects the two. Silent anxiety in teenage girls hides behind achievement and compliance.

item.title: The Worry That Grows Alongside Her
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: Nearly 1 in 5 pregnant women experiences clinically significant anxiety, yet standard screening tools were not designed to detect it.

item.title: The Invisible Weight After the Arrival
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: Postpartum anxiety affects up to 1 in 4 new mothers and has 75% comorbidity with postpartum depression. It hides behind good mothering.

item.title: The Dread That Has No Address
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: Anxiety is often the first symptom of perimenopause, arriving years before hot flashes. 15-50% of women are affected.

item.title: The Quiet After the Storm That Was Not Quiet at All
item.dc:creator: Ozaia
item.description: Menopausal symptoms can leave lasting effects on mental health into post-menopause. Anxiety research in older women remains substantially neglected.
